overlooked.headline: Section text spans lose inline HTML structure.
overlooked.detail: run.py captures only the text content between headings. Inline tables, lists, blockquotes, figures, and code blocks collapse to plain text in section_packets/*.md. 11_HTML_RENDER will need to rebuild structure from the original source HTML, not from these packets. The packets are faithful for body text but NOT a substitute for the source span.
overlooked.recommended_action: Document in README and have 11_HTML_RENDER consume section-map.json + raw source jointly, using source_offset_start/end as the slice boundary.

overlooked.headline: source_offset_start / source_offset_end are wired in the parser but never written to section objects.
overlooked.detail: HtmlSectionWalker tracks _char_offset and emits offsets on every event, but parse_html() does not record them on the emitted section dicts. Both fields are currently None on every section. If 14_LOOPBACK_REVIEW or 12_EXPORTS need byte-precise reattachment to the original source, the information is dropped.
overlooked.recommended_action: Capture the offset at heading_open and the next heading_open (or section_close) as the section's [start,end). Cheap fix; missed it.

overlooked.headline: Tab containers and main-flow content are flattened.
overlooked.detail: GTQ-03 contains parallel content under <section id="paper"> and tangent <section id="tangent-macarthur"> with the same h2 headings ("MacArthur and the Equation", "The Equation", etc.). The lane disambiguates section_ids with -2/-3 suffixes by source order, but loses the semantic distinction that one is the canonical body and the other is a tangent expansion. Downstream 09_GRAPH_LINKS will not know these are intentional duplicates.
overlooked.recommended_action: Add a section.role field {body|tangent|summary|media|rigor} derived from source_anchor prefix; preserve a tangent_of pointer to the canonical sibling.

attack_surfaces.headline: Equation count is fooled by literal dollar signs and inline single-symbol math.
attack_surfaces.detail: count_equations() matches `$...$` pairs. Text like 'cost was $5 vs $10' would register as one inline equation. Single-character math like '$s = -1$' inflates totals (GTQ-03 reports 192 equations; many are pathway labels, not derivations). An adversarial article quoting many currency figures could push 02 over the equation-mismatch loopback threshold.
attack_surfaces.exploitation: Author quotes 12 dollar amounts in a financial example, lane writes loopback to 14, 07_MATH_TRANSLATION halts.
attack_surfaces.mitigation: Require regex content to include an operator or LaTeX command between `$...$`, or trust 07 to filter and have 02 emit a more conservative count alongside the raw count.

attack_surfaces.headline: html.parser is forgiving to a fault. Mal-formed HTML can desync section/heading nesting.
attack_surfaces.detail: Word-exported HTML, RTF-converted pages, and AI-generated HTML often have unclosed tags or out-of-order `` markers. html.parser does not raise on these. The HtmlSectionWalker state machine (open_stack, section_anchor_stack) could end up with phantom open sections, producing inferred=false sections that should be inferred=true.
attack_surfaces.exploitation: Drop a Word-saved HTML article with an unclosed <section> tag. 02 emits a polluted heading tree without flagging the source as mal-formed.
attack_surfaces.mitigation: After parse, validate that section_count <= count of regex matches of `<h[1-6]` in source. If they diverge by >1, write a structural-noise loopback.

attack_surfaces.headline: section_id is heading-text-derived. Editorial rewording silently invalidates downstream joins.
attack_surfaces.detail: If David edits 'The Equation' to 'The Master Equation' between runs, every downstream packet keyed on `sec-002-the-equation` orphans. stable_uuid is computed from slug(heading_path), so it also changes. Re-runs lose the join.
attack_surfaces.exploitation: Innocuous editorial pass on headings between Round 1 and Round 2 silently breaks 04_TAGS, 05_CLAIMS, 08_SECTION_VECTORS, 10_RIGOR linkage.
attack_surfaces.mitigation: 13_LAYER_LEDGER should own a section_id_redirects table. On rerun, 02 emits {old_id, new_id, similarity_score, action} for each section that drifted, and downstream lanes consult the redirect table before treating an id as missing.

decision: All six are concrete; none triggered a loopback for the Round 1 deliverable. 02-O2 (source offsets) is the cheapest to fix and most load-bearing for 14_LOOPBACK_REVIEW — flagged as a next-iteration task on this lane rather than blocking testable status.
